narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team looking at your application like piecing together a story about your journey to the bar exam, and unfortunately, the chapters they've seen so far paint a picture of real challenges ahead, leading to our tough decision to reject your application based on a predicted 57% chance of not passing. Your Year 3 grades, landing you in the bottom decile of your school, stood out as the strongest signal—it's like the model saw that as your most recent and telling sign of struggling to keep up in a demanding law environment. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 19.3, which whispered doubts about the raw analytical firepower needed to tackle the bar's complexities. Your Year 1 decile of 1.0 reinforced this early pattern, suggesting those foundational hurdles haven't fully cleared. Coming from a lower-middle family income added another layer, as the model associates that with extra barriers to the intensive prep and resources many successful students rely on. Your Asian background, while a source of strength for many, in this predictive story tilted toward higher hurdles in the data we've seen. Even your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.2 and full-time commitment couldn't outweigh these, pulling gently but steadily in the same direction, alongside your gender as a male. It's not that any one piece defines you—it's how they all intertwined to form this cautious forecast. We truly see your potential and the effort you've put in, but this combination just didn't align with the profiles that historically cross the bar finish line. If you boost those deciles and LSAT through targeted practice, your story could rewrite itself for the next opportunity. Hang in there—you've got this drive, and with some adjustments, the narrative can shift.
